module.title: SAP TM Freight Settlement & Charge Management

learningObjectives.title: Understand memo adjustments
learningObjectives.description: Explain when and why credit/debit memos are created after settlement.

learningObjectives.title: Differentiate credit and debit
learningObjectives.description: Clarify payment reduction versus increase and related financial effects.

learningObjectives.title: Understand adjustment controls
learningObjectives.description: Describe dispute handling, audit documentation, and posting impacts.

sections.content: Credit Memo and Debit Memo processing in SAP Transportation Management allows transportation charges to be adjusted after freight settlement. A Credit Memo reduces the amount payable to a carrier, while a Debit Memo increases the payable amount. These adjustments ensure that carrier payments accurately reflect actual transportation services and contractual agreements.

sections.title: Key Concept
sections.content: Credit Memo decreases transportation charges. Debit Memo increases transportation charges.

A carrier submits an invoice including ₹8,000 as waiting charges that were not approved in the transportation contract. During invoice verification, SAP TM identifies the discrepancy and generates a Credit Memo to reduce the carrier payment by the incorrect amount.

sections.title: Best Practice
sections.content: Always document the business reason for every Credit Memo or Debit Memo. Proper documentation simplifies audits and carrier dispute resolution.

sections.title: Real Project Example
sections.content: A global automotive manufacturer automated Credit Memo generation for duplicate freight invoices. Incorrect carrier payments were reduced significantly while invoice processing became faster.

sections.title: Interview Tip
sections.content: Remember this simple rule: Credit Memo = Reduce Payment. Debit Memo = Increase Payment. This is frequently asked in SAP TM certification exams.

sections.title: Common Mistake
sections.content: Credit and Debit Memos adjust existing settlement amounts. They do not replace the original Freight Settlement Document.

items: Both documents adjust Freight Settlement results.
Invoice Verification often triggers Credit or Debit Memos.
Proper documentation improves financial transparency and auditing.
